question: Will CPI rise more than 1.0% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data was officially released on April 10, 2026, showing a 0.3% monthly increase, well below the 1.0% threshold required for YES resolution. Multiple authoritative sources confirm this figure, making the probability of YES resolution 0%. The market should resolve to NO.
key_factors: Official BLS data released showing 0.3% monthly CPI increase
Energy price impacts from Iran conflict were significant but insufficient to reach 1% threshold
Market closes April 10 at 12:25 PM, same day as data release
risks: Potential data revisions (though extremely unlikely to change by 0.7+ percentage points)
